name: the-lucifer-effect
description: Philip Zimbardo's The Lucifer Effect — social psychology toolkit exploring how situational forces transform good people into evil actors, based on the Stanford Prison Experiment and Abu Ghraib
useCase: Understand the Stanford Prison Experiment, learn how situational forces produce evil, recognize the fundamental attribution error, and develop resistance to unjust systems
knownRisks: Some scholars have criticized Zimbardo's interpretation of the SPE — alternative explanations exist
The book's detailed descriptions of abuse may be disturbing for some readers
Zimbardo's situational determinism may underemphasize individual responsibility
